description: Clean up config.json schema: (1) rename 'plugins' permission map key to 'permissions', (2) remove skipPermissions from config (keep only CLI flag + env var), (3) remove all legacy aliases (skipConfirmation, --dangerously-skip-confirmation, OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION). No backwards compatibility — hard rename, hard removal.

userStories.title: Rename config key 'plugins' to 'permissions' in types, parsing, and saving
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the config.json permission map key renamed from 'plugins' to 'permissions' so the schema clearly distinguishes plugin discovery (localPlugins) from plugin authorization (permissions).
acceptanceCriteria: OpentabsConfig interface field is 'permissions: Record<string, PluginPermissionConfig>' (not 'plugins')

The existing legacy migration that detects old 'permissions' with trustedDomains/sensitiveDomains is removed entirely (that migration code in parseConfigRecord around lines 175-183 for hasOldPermissions — remove it; the new 'permissions' key IS the permission map now)
The existing legacy migration that detects old 'plugins' as Array (lines 188-214) is removed entirely
The existing legacy migration that detects old 'tools' and 'browserToolPolicy' fields (lines 169-183) is removed entirely
The existing legacy migration that detects old 'npmPlugins' array (lines 216-226) is removed entirely
savePluginPermissions writes the 'permissions' key when saving config.json
loadConfig default config uses { localPlugins: [], permissions: {} }
All config.test.ts tests updated — remove all migration tests for old formats, update all fixtures to use 'permissions' key
userStories.notes:
Key file: platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts

This is a hard rename with no backwards compatibility. The config.json key changes from 'plugins' to 'permissions'. Anyone with an old config gets a parse that returns empty permissions — that's fine, they re-configure in the side panel.

Changes in config.ts:
1. OpentabsConfig interface (line 42-49): rename field 'plugins' to 'permissions'
2. parseConfigRecord (line 164-237): read 'record.permissions' via parsePluginsConfig(). Remove ALL legacy migration blocks: hasOldToolsFormat, hasOldBrowserToolPolicy, hasOldPermissions checks (lines 169-183), the Array.isArray(record.plugins) block (lines 188-214), and the npmPlugins migration (lines 216-226). The function becomes much simpler.
3. Return statement (line 236): { localPlugins, permissions, skipPermissions } — note: skipPermissions removal is a separate story
4. savePluginPermissions (around line 303-392): change 'plugins' to 'permissions' in the OpentabsConfig object construction (line 329)
5. loadConfig default (line 262): change 'plugins: {}' to 'permissions: {}'
6. The isLocalPathEntry helper (used only by the legacy Array migration) can be removed if it has no other callers.

In config.test.ts:
- Remove test 'migrates old-format config with tools/browserToolPolicy to empty plugins' (around line 108)
- Remove test 'migrates legacy plugins array with local paths' (if exists)
- Remove test 'migrates npmPlugins' (if exists)
- Update ALL remaining test fixtures: change '"plugins":' to '"permissions":' and 'config.plugins' to 'config.permissions'
- Update savePluginPermissions tests to expect 'permissions' key in written JSON

userStories.title: Remove skipPermissions from config and remove all legacy aliases
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want skipPermissions removed from the config file and all legacy aliases (skipConfirmation, --dangerously-skip-confirmation, OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION) removed so permission bypass is only available via the ephemeral --dangerously-skip-permissions flag or OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS=1 env var.
acceptanceCriteria: OpentabsConfig interface no longer has skipPermissions field
parseConfigRecord no longer reads skipPermissions or skipConfirmation from config
savePluginPermissions no longer preserves skipPermissions when saving
reload.ts computes skipPermissions solely from isCliSkipPermissions() — no config.skipPermissions
skip-permissions.ts only checks --dangerously-skip-permissions and OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS — no legacy aliases
No references to 'skipConfirmation' remain anywhere in platform/mcp-server/src/
No references to 'OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION' remain anywhere in platform/mcp-server/src/
No references to '--dangerously-skip-confirmation' remain anywhere in platform/mcp-server/src/
All config.test.ts tests for skipPermissions/skipConfirmation parsing removed
userStories.notes:
Key files:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts:
   - Remove 'skipPermissions?: boolean' from OpentabsConfig
   - Remove the skipPermissions/skipConfirmation parsing block (lines 228-234)
   - Remove skipPermissions from return (line 236) — after US-001 this returns { localPlugins, permissions }
   - Remove 'skipPermissions: current.skipPermissions' from savePluginPermissions (line 330)

2. platform/mcp-server/src/skip-permissions.ts (entire file, ~24 lines):
   - Remove process.argv.includes('--dangerously-skip-confirmation')
   - Remove process.env.OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION === '1'
   - Remove comments about legacy aliases
   - Keep ONLY: process.argv.includes('--dangerously-skip-permissions') and process.env.OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS === '1'

3. platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts:
   - Line 224: Change `isCliSkipPermissions() || config.skipPermissions === true` to just `isCliSkipPermissions()`

4. platform/mcp-server/src/config.test.ts:
   - Remove 'preserves skipPermissions flag' test
   - Remove 'migrates legacy skipConfirmation to skipPermissions' test

state.skipPermissions, isCliSkipPermissions(), and all runtime skip-permission logic STAYS — only the config file path and legacy aliases are removed.

userStories.title: Update all server-side consumers of config.plugins to config.permissions
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want all server-side code that accessed config.plugins updated to config.permissions.
acceptanceCriteria: All reload.test.ts test config JSON fixtures use 'permissions' key
userStories.notes:
Mechanical rename.

platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts:
- Line 221: `{ ...config.plugins }` → `{ ...config.permissions }`
- Line 241: `Object.keys(config.plugins).length` → `Object.keys(config.permissions).length`

platform/mcp-server/src/reload.test.ts:
- All test config JSON that writes '"plugins": { ... }' → '"permissions": { ... }' (around lines 169-171, 176, 202, 225, 231)

platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.test.ts:
- Line 450: `expect(body).not.toHaveProperty('plugins')` — check context; if this asserts the HTTP response doesn't leak config, it may need updating to check 'permissions' instead, or both.

state.pluginPermissions stays unchanged — do NOT rename it.

userStories.title: Update CLI config commands for 'permissions' key
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want CLI config commands to read/write the 'permissions' key.
acceptanceCriteria: All CLI config.test.ts tests updated and passing
userStories.notes:
Mechanical rename in platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts:
- Line 54: CANONICAL_CONFIG_SECTIONS = ['localPlugins', 'permissions']
- Line 58: comment
- Line 71: normalized.permissions = config.permissions ?? {}
- Lines 124, 126: key === 'permissions' and pc.cyan('permissions')
- Lines 195-224: ensurePluginEntry and pruneEmptyPluginEntries — all config.plugins → config.permissions

platform/cli/src/commands/config.test.ts: all 'plugins' → 'permissions' in test fixtures.

userStories.title: Update E2E fixtures and tests for 'permissions' key and remove skipPermissions config references
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests updated to use the new config schema.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/fixtures.ts OpentabsConfig uses 'permissions' not 'plugins', no skipPermissions field
All E2E test files use config.permissions instead of config.plugins
E2E tests that set skipPermissions in config objects are updated — skipPermissions must come from env var only, not config
userStories.notes:
Files to update:

e2e/fixtures.ts:
- Line 195: interface field 'plugins?' → 'permissions?'
- Line 200: default value — remove skipPermissions if present; rename plugins to permissions
- Check if the fixture writes skipPermissions into config.json — if so, remove it. The E2E tests use OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS env var (line 514) which is the correct mechanism.

e2e/permissions.e2e.ts: ~10 instances of config.plugins → config.permissions. Some tests may also reference skipPermissions in config — remove those, ensure they use the env var instead.

e2e/plugin-management.e2e.ts: ~4 instances of config.plugins

e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts: ~5 instances of config.plugins. Also check lines 232-233 and 275-276 for skipPermissions references.

e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts: ~2 instances of config.plugins. Lines 515-540 have skipPermissions test — this tests the 'PERMISSIONS BYPASSED' banner which is driven by the server state (set from env var), so the test should still work; just make sure the config object doesn't set skipPermissions.

e2e/side-panel-realtime-state.e2e.ts: check lines 50, 231-232 for skipPermissions.

Critical: The E2E fixtures likely write config.json to disk for each test. Make sure the written JSON uses 'permissions' key and does NOT include skipPermissions. Tests that need skip behavior should use OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS=1 env var (most already do).

userStories.title: Update documentation for config schema changes
userStories.description: As a user reading the docs, I want accurate documentation of the current config schema.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx: config table shows 'permissions' as the plugin permission map key
skipPermissions row removed from config table
Example config uses 'permissions' key, no skipPermissions
The old '### permissions' section (trustedDomains/sensitiveDomains) is replaced with documentation of the new permissions key (Record<string, PluginPermissionConfig>)
Legacy alias mentions removed from env var table and all prose
CLAUDE.md: 'under the `plugins` map' updated to 'under the `permissions` map'
docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx: --dangerously-skip-permissions docs kept, no config equivalent mentioned
The build-plugin skill file (.claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md) updated if it references skipPermissions in config
userStories.notes:
Key files:

1. docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx — this is the big one:
   - Config table (lines 40-62): remove 'browserToolPolicy' row, remove old 'permissions' row (trustedDomains stuff), rename 'plugins' to 'permissions' if it exists as a row (check — it may not have a row for the current 'plugins' key yet), remove 'skipPermissions' row
   - Example config (lines 68-97): the example shows OLD format with 'tools', 'browserToolPolicy', old 'permissions' — replace entirely with current schema: { localPlugins, permissions: { pluginName: { permission, tools } } }
   - '### permissions' section (lines 169-230): this documents the OLD trustedDomains/sensitiveDomains system which no longer exists. Replace with documentation of the NEW permissions key: Record<string, { permission?: 'off'|'ask'|'auto', tools?: Record<string, 'off'|'ask'|'auto'> }>
   - Line 241-243: remove 'skipPermissions: true in config' and all legacy alias mentions
   - Line 286-288: env var table — update OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS description to remove 'Equivalent to skipPermissions: true in config' and remove OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION alias mention
   - Remove any mention of the '### Legacy fields' or migration section if it exists

2. CLAUDE.md (root, line 93): 'under the `plugins` map' → 'under the `permissions` map'

3. docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx (line 73): keep --dangerously-skip-permissions, remove any mention of config file equivalent

4. .claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md: check if it mentions skipPermissions in config context

5. platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md: check for any 'plugins' config key references
